title: End of module: Forces and Newton's Laws of Motion

questions.text: A resultant force of $F = 15\,\text{N}$ acts on a particle of mass $m = 3\,\text{kg}$. Find its acceleration $a$.
A particle of mass $m = 8\,\text{kg}$ accelerates at $a = 2.5\,\text{m s}^{-2}$. Find the magnitude of the resultant force $F$ acting on it.
Find the weight, in newtons, of an object of mass $50\,\text{kg}$. Use $g = 9.81\,\text{m s}^{-2}$.
An object has a weight of $294.3\,\text{N}$. Find its mass, in kg. Use $g = 9.81\,\text{m s}^{-2}$.

A passenger of mass $70\,\text{kg}$ stands on a lift floor. The lift accelerates upward at $2\,\text{m s}^{-2}$. Using $g = 9.81\,\text{m s}^{-2}$, find the normal reaction force the floor exerts on the passenger.
questions.explanation: Taking up as positive, Newton's second law gives $R - mg = ma$, so $R = m(g+a) = 70 \times (9.81 + 2) = 70 \times 11.81 = 826.7\,\text{N}$.

questions.text: The same $70\,\text{kg}$ passenger is in a lift that instead accelerates downward at $2\,\text{m s}^{-2}$. Using $g = 9.81\,\text{m s}^{-2}$, find the normal reaction force from the floor.
questions.explanation: Taking up as positive and the acceleration as $-2\,\text{m s}^{-2}$: $R - mg = m(-a)$, so $R = m(g-a) = 70 \times (9.81 - 2) = 70 \times 7.81 = 546.7\,\text{N}$.

questions.text: A particle is released from rest on a smooth (frictionless) plane inclined at $30^\circ$ to the horizontal. Using $g = 9.81\,\text{m s}^{-2}$, find its acceleration down the slope.
questions.explanation: Resolving along the slope with no friction: $ma = mg\sin\theta$, so $a = g\sin\theta = 9.81 \times \sin 30^\circ = 9.81 \times 0.5 = 4.905\,\text{m s}^{-2}$.

questions.text: A particle of mass $10\,\text{kg}$ rests on a smooth plane inclined at $40^\circ$ to the horizontal, held by a string along the slope. Using $g = 9.81\,\text{m s}^{-2}$, find the normal reaction force.
questions.explanation: Perpendicular to the slope the particle is in equilibrium, so $R = mg\cos\theta = 10 \times 9.81 \times \cos 40^\circ = 98.1 \times 0.766 = 75.1\,\text{N}$ (to 3 s.f.).

questions.text: Two perpendicular forces of $9\,\text{N}$ and $12\,\text{N}$ act on a particle. Find the magnitude of the resultant force.
questions.explanation: The forces are perpendicular, so by Pythagoras' theorem $R = \sqrt{9^2 + 12^2} = \sqrt{81+144} = \sqrt{225} = 15\,\text{N}$.

questions.text: Which statement correctly describes Newton's first law of motion?
choices: A body accelerates in proportion to the resultant force acting on it
A body remains at rest or moves with constant velocity unless acted on by a resultant force
Every action has an equal and opposite reaction
Force equals mass times velocity
questions.explanation: Newton's first law states that a body's velocity only changes when a resultant (net) external force acts on it — otherwise it stays at rest or keeps moving in a straight line at constant speed.

questions.text: A book rests on a table and is in equilibrium. Which pair of forces are equal and opposite in this situation?
choices: The book's weight and the normal reaction from the table, because they are a Newton's-third-law pair
The book's weight and the normal reaction from the table, because the book is in equilibrium (not because of Newton's third law)
There are no forces acting on the book since it is not moving
The normal reaction and the force the book exerts on the Earth
questions.explanation: Weight and normal reaction act on the *same* body (the book) and happen to balance because the book is in vertical equilibrium — that is Newton's first law at work, not the third. A true third-law pair always acts on two different bodies.

questions.text: A car engine exerts a driving force on the road via its tyres. By Newton's third law, what force does the road exert on the car?
choices: An equal and opposite force on the car, forward along the road
A force of the same type and magnitude, but acting on the engine rather than the car
No force at all, since the road does not move
A force equal to the car's weight, acting vertically
questions.explanation: By Newton's third law, if the tyres push backward on the road, the road pushes forward on the tyres (the car) with an equal and opposite force — this reaction force from the road is what propels the car forward.

questions.text: A particle is in equilibrium under the action of exactly three coplanar forces. What must be true?
choices: All three forces must have equal magnitude
The vector sum of the three forces must be zero
Each force must be perpendicular to the other two
One of the forces must be zero
questions.explanation: Equilibrium means the resultant (vector sum) of all forces acting on the particle is zero; the individual forces can have any magnitudes and directions as long as they sum to zero.

questions.text: Which of the following correctly gives the SI unit of force in base units?
choices: $\text{kg}\,\text{m}^{-1}\,\text{s}^{2}$
$\text{kg}\,\text{m}\,\text{s}^{-2}$
$\text{kg}\,\text{m}^{2}\,\text{s}^{-1}$
$\text{kg}\,\text{m}\,\text{s}^{-1}$
questions.explanation: From $F = ma$, the newton is $1\,\text{N} = 1\,\text{kg}\,\text{m}\,\text{s}^{-2}$: mass in kg times acceleration in $\text{m s}^{-2}$.
